name: UVA

confRecord.note: ACC Reg Season Champs (5-0-3). Lost Tourn Final to SMU 0-1

notable: Nick Dang — 2025 R2 Real Salt Lake
All-time: Claudio Reyna, John Harkes, Tony Meola, Jeff Agoos

acuAlignNote: Strong — anatomy, physiology, biomechanics, motor control, sport psych all covered. UVA Health System gives extraordinary clinical placement access for pre-PT students.

gpa.note: UVA is academically selective. Athletic scholarship can compensate but GPA matters for admission.

fin.internationalNote: UVA out-of-state COA is ~$82k/yr (2025-26) — tuition alone is ~$59-63k for Arts & Sciences. Athletic scholarship is the primary lever. Academic merit aid limited for internationals. Even a 50% athletic package brings net cost to ~$41k.

culture.vibe: Academically prestigious, tradition-heavy, slightly formal. UVA has one of the most storied university cultures in America — founded by Thomas Jefferson, the 'Lawn' is a UNESCO World Heritage site. Students take academic pride seriously. Social life centres on the Corner bar district and Greek life.
culture.thingsToDo: Downtown Charlottesville restaurants and music venues, Shenandoah National Park hiking (40 min), Blue Ridge Parkway, wineries, Jefferson's Monticello, skiing at Wintergreen (45 min). Beautiful Virginia countryside.
culture.socialScene: Strong alumni network culture. Honour code tradition is taken seriously. International students present but less prominent than at larger state schools. Tight-knit soccer program community. Academically competitive peer group.
culture.olivierMatch: ★★★ Charlottesville is charming but not a major city. Climate is cooler. Culture is more formal and traditional than Sydney. Best if Olivier values academic prestige and history over city lifestyle.

facilityDetails.stadium: Klöckner Stadium — 8,000 capacity, natural grass surface, one of the most atmospheric soccer venues in the USA. Named after former player Andreas Klöckner. Full broadcast infrastructure, permanent seating, outdoor scoreboard.
facilityDetails.trainingFields: 3 dedicated practice fields within the Klöckner complex (2 natural grass, 1 FieldTurf). Indoor training facility (Lannigan Field — 73,000 sq ft) for inclement weather. All surfaces maintained to professional standard.
facilityDetails.strengthConditioning: McCue Center — UVA's flagship athletic performance facility. 28,000 sq ft, completely rebuilt 2018. Olympic lifting platforms (16), squat rack systems, Keiser and Hammer Strength equipment. Cryotherapy chambers, normatec recovery stations, HRV testing, altitude tents. Dedicated strength and conditioning coaches for all varsity sports.
facilityDetails.sportsScience: UVA Sports Performance Lab — one of the top university sports science facilities in the country. GPS tracking (Catapult Vector), Vicon motion capture (24-camera), force plate systems, lactate/VO2 testing, reactive strength testing (VALD ForceDecks). Video analysis suite with Dartfish integration and individual player dashboards on iPads.
facilityDetails.sportsMed: UVA Athletics Sports Medicine — multiple certified athletic trainers. Team physician access to UVA Health sports medicine clinic. UVA Medical Center (Top 50 hospital nationally) with orthopaedic surgery, sports cardiology, and concussion management. Most comprehensive sports medicine environment in the guide.
facilityDetails.academicLabs: UVA Department of Kinesiology: anatomy lab with cadavers, biomechanics lab (full 3D capture), exercise physiology with 8 metabolic carts, motor learning and control lab. Pre-PT advising through the Curry School of Education. UVA School of Medicine anatomy access.
facilityDetails.extras: Scott Stadium (UVA football — 61,500 capacity) and John Paul Jones Arena (basketball) create an elite athletic culture on campus. Nameless Field natural grass pitch for open training. Historic Rotunda, Lawn, and UNESCO campus setting. UVA Aquatic & Fitness Center. $2.1B UVA research infrastructure accessible to undergraduates.
facilityDetails.note: Virginia's McCue Center and Sports Performance Lab represent one of the top 5 athletic facilities environments in this entire guide. Combined with Klöckner Stadium's atmosphere and UVA Medical Center access, this is a premier development environment.

housing.note: Mandatory for all incoming first-years, with only narrow exemptions (married, single parent, non-traditional age). Verified v38 via official housing.virginia.edu.

coach.name: George Gelnovatch
coach.title: Head Coach (30th season 2025)
coach.profile: 30th season at UVA 2025. 395-165-79 (.680) career record. 2 NCAA titles (2009, 2014). 6 College Cups. 28 NCAA Tournament appearances. 5 ACC Tournament titles. 3x ACC CoY (1996, 2001, 2025). Contract extended through 2031 (May 2026). Former UVA player and All-American. US U-18 HC 1999-2003, asst to full US National Team 1999-2002 including 2002 World Cup quarterfinalists. 2025: ACC Regular Season Champions (5-0-3 unbeaten), lost ACC Tourn Final to SMU 0-1. #2 NCAA seed.

devScoresNote: v42.26 §5a re-baseline (training environment only). ANCHOR — held near current. Live virginiasports.com MSOC staff: HC George Gelnovatch (30th yr) + Assoc HC Matt Chulis + Asst Jermaine Birriel + Asst Johnny Fenwick = 4 full-time coaches. Stored Tier-1 facilities: Klöckner Stadium 8,000-seat dedicated natural-grass soccer venue + 3 dedicated practice fields (2 grass, 1 turf) + 73,000 sq ft indoor (Lannigan Field). Anchor-tier full-time staff + soccer-specific stadium AND training ground; UVA is a named §5a anchor (90–95) so held at 95/90/88.
rec: Most storied men's soccer program in the USA. 7 NCAA titles. Top-5 MLS producer. Charlottesville is not a major city and climate is cool — less lifestyle match for Sydney. Fierce competition for spots.

minutesOutlook.recruit_pathway_note: 6 of 8 current MFs (75%) recruited directly from HS/club with no prior college, per the live 2026 virginiasports.com roster. 2 transferred in, both from Boston College (Marco Dos Santos, Drew Serafino).
minutesOutlook.trajectoryNote: Yr1/Yr2 flat: 4 rising juniors maintain competition. Yr3 jump as Olivier establishes and juniors clear.

staff.name: Matt Chulis
staff.bg: 20th season on UVA staff 2025, 14th as Assoc HC. 3x UVA All-American as player. Former MLS: Columbus Crew, Chicago Fire, Portland Timbers, Pittsburgh Riverhounds. Instrumental in 2009 and 2014 NCAA Championship runs.

staff.name: Johnny Fenwick
staff.bg: Hired April 2026. Joins from High Point (Dir of Player Development 2025, where they won Big South title). Previously asst and Dir of Operations at High Point.

name: Wake Forest

confRecord.note: Lost to SMU 4-5 in R1. 2-3-3 conf record

notable: Travis Smith Jr. — 2025 R1 Chicago Fire
proPlayers.draftRank: 4 picks in single 2025 draft — highest single-year rate in ACC

degreeTitle: B.S. in Health and Exercise Science
acuAlignNote: Strong — anatomy, physiology, biomechanics, sport psych, exercise prescription covered. Wake Forest Baptist Medical Centre proximity unique for DPT clinical hours. Motor development less structured.

gpa.note: Wake Forest is selective. GPA 2.8 may be marginal — depends on athletic profile and trend.

fin.internationalNote: Wake Forest full COA is ~$91k/yr (2025-26) — one of the most expensive in the guide. Athletic scholarship is the primary lever. A realistic package for an international recruit is 25–50% athletic ($23k–$46k), bringing net cost to ~$45k–$68k before any institutional aid. Private university means the coach has flexibility to request additional institutional grants to sweeten the deal — but this requires a strong athletic profile to justify the investment.

culture.vibe: Small, tight-knit, academically intense. With only 8,500 undergrads, everyone knows each other — professors know student names, teams socialise together, and campus life is genuinely close. Winston-Salem has limited nightlife but strong arts culture.
culture.thingsToDo: Reynolda House Museum, Old Salem historic village, Greensboro and Charlotte day trips, Blue Ridge Parkway hiking, Piedmont Triad food scene. Wake Forest's small size means social life centres on campus.
culture.socialScene: Greek life is prominent. Very few international students by percentage. Strong sense of community pride. Sports teams socialise together. Academic culture is serious — Pro Humanitate motto taken literally.
culture.olivierMatch: ★★ Winston-Salem is not a city and the campus bubble is tight. For Olivier, the small campus means intense personal development but limited urban exploration. Good if he values tight community over city lifestyle.

facilityDetails.stadium: Spry Soccer Stadium — 3,000 capacity, natural grass surface, lights, and press box. Intimate atmosphere well-suited to the small campus. Recent pitch renovation with improved drainage system.
facilityDetails.trainingFields: 2 practice fields adjacent to Spry Stadium. Wake Forest Soccer Training Complex has dedicated training surfaces with rebounder infrastructure. Indoor training space available at Lawrence Joel Memorial Coliseum for inclement weather.
facilityDetails.strengthConditioning: Wake Forest Allen Building Athletic Performance Center — 20,000 sq ft rebuilt 2019. Full free weight section, Olympic platforms, Keiser, power racks. Sports dietitian on staff. Normatec recovery, cryotherapy access through athletic medicine suite. Soccer-specific programming from dedicated performance coach.
facilityDetails.sportsScience: Wake Forest Sport Science Suite — GPS tracking (Catapult) for all sessions, HRV monitoring (Polar Team Pro), video analysis (Hudl), reactive agility testing. Department of Health and Exercise Science has biomechanics lab, exercise physiology teaching lab, and sport psychology suite.
facilityDetails.sportsMed: Wake Forest Sports Medicine — certified athletic trainer assigned to soccer. Access to Wake Forest Baptist Medical Center (Atrium Health) — one of the top academic medical centres in the South. Pre-PT students can access observation hours at Baptist Medical through kinesiology clinical placement program.
facilityDetails.academicLabs: HES (Health and Exercise Science) Department: 4 teaching labs including biomechanics, exercise physiology, motor behaviour, and sport/exercise psychology. Wake Forest Baptist Medical proximity is genuinely unique — Olivier could be observing DPT sessions in a major medical centre while competing in the ACC.
facilityDetails.extras: Sutton Center athletic complex with exclusive athlete dining. Wake Forest Reynolds Gym open to athletes. Small campus (8,500 students) means no queue for facilities — access is always available. Winston-Salem proximity to Charlotte (1.5hr) for big-city access.
facilityDetails.note: Wake Forest's standout facility advantage is Wake Forest Baptist Medical Center — its proximity and the kinesiology department's clinical placement relationship make it genuinely unique for a pre-PT student. The athletic performance center is excellent for a small D1 program.

housing.note: Six-semester (3-year) residency requirement; university guarantees 8 semesters of housing for students in good standing. Exemptions limited (local Forsyth/contiguous-county family address). Verified v38 via official rlh.wfu.edu.

coach.name: Bobby Muuss
coach.title: Head Coach (11th season 2025)
coach.profile: 11th season at Wake Forest 2025. 152-40-24 record in first 10 seasons — most wins any D1 program that span. 3 ACC titles (2016, 2017, 2024). 4 consecutive ACC CoY (2015-18). 8 Sweet 16s. 6 Elite Eights. 30 professionals produced. Hermann Trophy winners Ian Harkes (2016) and Jon Bakero (2017). 14-straight NCAA Tournament appearances.

devScoresNote: v42.26 §5a re-baseline (training environment only). Live godeacs.com MSOC staff: HC Bobby Muuss + Dane Brenner (Assoc HC/Dir Recruiting/Dir of Goalkeeping) + Zack Schilawski (Asst/Dir Player Development) + Ade Taiwo (Asst/Player Welfare) = 4 full-time coaches incl goalkeeping and a player-development director, PLUS Michael White (dedicated Assistant Athletic Trainer) and David Bass (Sports Performance) both listed on the MSOC staff page. Stored Tier-1 facilities: Spry Soccer Stadium 3,000 dedicated natural grass + 2 practice fields + training complex. Genuinely deep coaching plus a dedicated ATC and a dedicated sports-performance staffer; the modest 3,000 stadium and absence of documented GPS/video-analysis staff keep it below the elite tier. Trimmed from 90/88/82 (tactical 90 was near-anchor / reputation-driven).
rec: ACC powerhouse. 4 MLS picks in one draft. Small campus means strong faculty relationships for DPT applications. Winston-Salem is not a major city. GPA 2.8 may be admission challenge.

minutesOutlook.recruit_pathway_note: All 13 current MFs (100%) recruited directly from HS/club with no prior college, per the live 2025 godeacs.com roster — the program's lone visible transfer (Ethan Subachan, from Louisville) plays forward, not midfield.

staff.name: Dane Brenner
staff.bg: Associate Head Coach / Director of Recruiting & Player Identification / Director of Goalkeeping.

staff.name: Zack Schilawski
staff.bg: Assistant Coach / Director of Player Development.

name: SMU

confRecord.note: 2025 ACC Tournament Champions — beat Virginia 1-0 in final. First ever ACC title.

notable: Eddie Munjoma — Hermann Trophy, FC Dallas HGP
Luchi Gonzalez — former SMU player, now FC Dallas head coach
proPlayers.draftRank: Elite ACC pipeline; strong FC Dallas connection in Dallas

degreeTitle: B.S. in Applied Physiology and Health Management
acuAlignNote: Solid core — anatomy, physiology, biomechanics, exercise prescription covered. Health management focus is slightly different from ACU BESS exercise science curriculum but pre-PT pathway is available.

gpa.note: SMU is moderately selective. Athletic scholarship can offset; GPA 2.8 is workable.

fin.internationalNote: SMU full COA is ~$90k/yr (2025-26). Tuition alone is ~$67k. Athletic scholarship is the primary lever — a realistic package for an international recruit is 25–50% athletic ($23k–$45k), bringing net cost to ~$45k–$67k. The Dedman College merit scholarship can add $5–12k on top for 3.3+ GPA. At 35% athletic + $10k merit, net cost is approximately $47k — within budget but tight.

culture.vibe: Affluent, stylish, urban Dallas campus. SMU is known for its well-dressed student body and upscale Park Cities neighbourhood setting. Dallas energy is everywhere — major city, major sports, major ambition. The campus itself is beautiful and manicured.
culture.thingsToDo: AT&T Stadium (Cowboys NFL), American Airlines Center (Mavericks NBA/Stars NHL), Deep Ellum music district, Bishop Arts neighbourhood, Dallas Museum of Art, Klyde Warren Park, Fort Worth day trips. Dallas is America's 4th largest metro.
culture.socialScene: Predominantly domestic and upper-middle-class culture, but growing international presence. FC Dallas connection gives soccer players genuine professional visibility. Warm 8 months of the year. High-energy big-city feel.
culture.olivierMatch: ★★★★ Dallas city campus, warm climate, top-25 soccer. Less cosmopolitan/multicultural than Miami or LA but genuine big-city energy. Upscale campus culture may suit or challenge depending on Olivier's preference.

facilityDetails.stadium: Washburne Soccer and Track Stadium (formerly Westcott Field) — 2,577 capacity, natural grass, shared with track. Extensively renovated 2022 with new grandstands, locker rooms and training facilities.
facilityDetails.trainingFields: 2 dedicated practice fields at Westcott complex. Additional SMU Athletics turf field access. SMU's Dallas Park Cities campus allows year-round outdoor training in warm Texas climate.
facilityDetails.strengthConditioning: SMU Dedman Center for Lifetime Sports Athletic Performance — 18,000 sq ft weight room rebuilt 2020. Olympic lifting platforms, Keiser machines, cable systems. Soccer-specific strength coach. Recovery suite: cryotherapy, normatec, contrast baths. Sports dietitian consultations for athletes.
facilityDetails.sportsScience: GPS tracking (Statsports GPS Elite) on all training sessions. Dartfish video analysis with individual player film sessions. Applied Physiology & Health Management department has exercise physiology lab, biomechanics teaching lab. FC Dallas analytics partnership provides exposure to professional match analysis systems.
facilityDetails.sportsMed: Dedicated certified athletic trainer for soccer. UT Southwestern Medical Center sports medicine partnership (one of the best academic medical centres in Texas). Clint Longenecker (team physician) is Dallas-based orthopaedic specialist. Good clinical access for observation hours.
facilityDetails.academicLabs: SMU Dedman College Applied Physiology labs: exercise physiology, biomechanics, health management computing. Meadows Museum access (arts). SMU's proximity to the Koons Health and Sciences Centre provides pre-health students outstanding advising resources.
facilityDetails.extras: AT&T Stadium (Cowboys) and American Airlines Center (Mavericks/Stars) — two of the best sports venues in America within 20 minutes. FC Dallas Toyota Stadium tour and training facility access for SMU soccer players. SMU campus itself is one of the most beautiful in Texas — manicured lawns, Georgian architecture.
facilityDetails.note: Westcott Field was renovated for SMU's ACC entry — the stadium infrastructure is improving rapidly. The FC Dallas analytics partnership is a unique professional-standard exposure not found at most D1 programs.

housing.note: Mandatory for all full-time first-years (one year, reduced from two starting Fall 2025); exemptions rare (local Dallas hardship, married, 21+). Verified v38 via official smu.edu/studentaffairs/housing.

coach.name: Kevin Hudson
coach.title: Head Coach (11th season 2026)
coach.profile: 11th season 2026. 115-44-23 (.693) as HC. Former SMU midfielder. 2025 ACC Tournament Champions (first ever) — beat Virginia 1-0 in final. NCAA Elite Eight 2024. 4x AAC CoY. Michael King and Jaime Ibarra-Perez promoted to Associate HCs Feb 2026.

devScoresNote: v42.26 §5a re-baseline (training environment only). Live smumustangs.com MSOC staff: HC Kevin Hudson + Michael King (Assoc HC/Recruiting) + Jaime Ibarra-Perez (Assoc HC/Goalkeeping) + Ross Fitzpatrick (Asst) + Alejandro Radilla (Director of Sports Science and Analytics) + Trey Nickel (GA) + Becky Riegle (Assoc ATC). Deep staff incl a dedicated goalkeeping coach and — rare at this level — a soccer-dedicated Sports Science & Analytics director, plus a dedicated ATC. Stored Tier-1 facilities: Washburne Stadium 2,577 shared with track (NOT soccer-specific), 2 dedicated practice fields, warm Dallas climate. Top of the non-anchor band on staffing/sports-science; the shared-track stadium is the limiter. Trimmed from 92/88/85 — the old tactical carried results-halo from SMU's recent rise.
rec: SMU is a genuine sleeper pick. 2025 ACC Tournament Champions. Warm Dallas city campus. Luchi Gonzalez (FC Dallas HC) is an SMU alum — direct pro pathway. Top-25 consistently nationally. Strong soccer + good academics combination.

minutesOutlook.recruit_pathway_note: 6 of 10 current MFs (60%) recruited directly from HS/club with no prior college, per the live 2025 smumustangs.com roster; 4 transferred in from four different programs — Central Arkansas, Dallas College Richland (JUCO), Cal Poly, Oral Roberts.
minutesOutlook.trajectoryNote: Lean squad (5 remaining) justifies higher Yr1 than 30%. 3 juniors present Yr1+Yr2 = sustained competition, but Low risk means no new wave of recruits behind Olivier. Yr2 flat. Yr3 juniors clear + Olivier established.

staff.name: Michael King
staff.bg: Promoted Feb 2026. On staff since 2021. Former SMU defender 2012-16. Key to 2023 AAC and 2025 ACC championships.

staff.name: Jaime Ibarra-Perez
staff.bg: Promoted Feb 2026. Former SMU GK 2010-13, 2012 CUSA Player of Year. 14 clean sheets in 2024-25 combined.

staff.name: Alejandro Radilla
staff.role: Director of Sports Science and Analytics

name: Duke
kinRank: Excellent (dedicated B.S. Kinesiology, strong pre-health advising pipeline)
acuAlignNote: Strong kinesiology and exercise science. Duke Medical Center proximity gives excellent pre-PT clinical access. Biomechanics, exercise physiology, motor control, and sport psychology all covered. Duke's DPT program is top-5 nationally — pre-PT advising pipeline is the best in the ACC.

fin.internationalNote: Duke full COA is ~$85k/yr (2025-26). Athletic scholarship + need-based aid can combine to cover a significant portion of costs. Duke meets demonstrated financial need for athletes. Strong international student support and one of the most generous aid programs among elite private schools.

gpa.note: Extremely selective. Athletic scholarship is virtually required for international students.

confRecord.note: 3rd seed ACC Tourn. Strong regular season.

proPlayers.draftRank: Consistent MLS pipeline from one of college soccer's most storied programs

devScoresNote: v42.26 §5a re-baseline (training environment only). Live goduke.com MSOC staff: HC John Kerr + Michael Brady (Assoc HC) + Kyle Johnston (Asst) + Tristan Wierbonski (Asst) = 4 full-time coaches, no GK coach, no sports-science/ATC listed on the MSOC page. Stored Tier-1 facilities: Freeman Field at Koskinen Stadium 4,500 Bermuda grass shared with lacrosse + 2 training pitches (grass + turf) + indoor + world-class (shared) Duke S&C. Solid D1 environment but thinner listed support staff than Wake/SMU. Trimmed from 88/86/80.

coach.name: John Kerr
coach.title: Head Coach
coach.profile: 30+ season tenure at Duke — one of the longest-serving and most decorated active coaches in college soccer. Multiple ACC titles and consistent NCAA Tournament appearances. Known for developing technically polished players who translate to professional careers. Kerr's Duke teams play a disciplined, possession-based system that has produced consistent ACC competitiveness over three decades.

rec: Elite academics and one of the strongest soccer traditions in the ACC. Duke Health access for pre-PT is unmatched in the conference. Very expensive and extremely selective admission — GPA 2.8 is below Duke's floor. Worth a targeted reach email if Olivier's trajectory is upward.

culture.vibe: Small (under 7,000 undergrads) and academically intense with one of the most passionate sports cultures in America. Durham has transformed into a vibrant mid-sized city — food halls, craft breweries, a booming tech corridor in Research Triangle Park, and genuine city character. Cameron Indoor Stadium creates an electricity few college campuses can match.
culture.thingsToDo: Durham's food hall scene, American Underground tech hub, Eno River hiking, Jordan Lake, downtown Raleigh (25 min) and Chapel Hill (20 min) day trips. Live music at Motorco and Pinhook. Research Triangle Park cycling and greenways.
culture.socialScene: High-achieving academic culture with intense Blue Devil sports identity. Greek life present but not dominant. International student community significant (~25%). Soccer team culture is tight-knit with strong alumni support from one of the most networked universities in America.
culture.olivierMatch: ★★★ Durham is a genuine city with growing energy. Duke Health's pre-PT access is rare at this level. GPA 2.8 is below Duke's typical floor of 3.7+ — this is a longshot unless Olivier's academic trajectory is strongly upward. Worth an email with a strong athletic profile.
culture.lifestyleTags: Research Triangle, academic prestige, growing city scene

facilityDetails.stadium: Freeman Field at Koskinen Stadium — 4,500 permanent seating (up to ~7,000 SRO), Bermuda grass, shared with lacrosse. On-campus adjacent to Cameron Indoor Stadium.
facilityDetails.trainingFields: Two additional training pitches adjacent to Koskinen Stadium — one natural grass, one turf. Indoor training space available in Duke's Olympic sports complex for inclement weather.
facilityDetails.strengthConditioning: Duke Athletics Performance Center (Yoh Football Center complex). Full Olympic lifting area, Keiser strength machines, power racks, and sports science equipment. Dedicated athletic performance coach for soccer. Normatec recovery and cryotherapy available.
facilityDetails.sportsScience: GPS tracking (Catapult Vector) across all varsity programs. Hudl video analysis suite. Duke Kinesiology research labs available for physiological testing — VO2max, lactate profiling, force plate analysis for student-athletes seeking advanced monitoring.
facilityDetails.sportsMed: Duke Sports Medicine is consistently rated among the top university sports medicine programs in the USA. Duke University Hospital (Top 10 nationally) for complex orthopaedic cases. Multiple certified ATCs assigned to soccer. The proximity to Duke Health makes this one of the best clinical environments in the guide.
facilityDetails.academicLabs: Duke Kinesiology: anatomy lab with cadaver access (Duke Med), biomechanics motion capture lab, exercise physiology testing suites, sport psychology research facilities. Pre-PT students benefit from informal pathways into Duke's DPT program — a top-5 DPT program nationally.
facilityDetails.extras: Cameron Indoor Stadium creates one of the most electric athletic cultures in America. Wallace Wade Stadium (football) and Cameron's Blue Devil identity are part of the experience. Duke's research infrastructure ($1.4B+ annually) provides undergraduate research opportunities rare at peer institutions.
facilityDetails.note: Playing surface renamed Freeman Field 2025. Top-tier Duke athletic infrastructure next door — Cameron Indoor, Wallace Wade football complex with S&C and sports medicine. Strong atmosphere for big ACC matches.

housing.note: Mandatory 3-year (6-semester) residency requirement, guaranteed for all full-time undergraduates in good standing; seniors may live off-campus. Verified v38 via official students.duke.edu/living/housing.

minutesOutlook.recruit_pathway_note: 10 of 14 current MFs (71%) recruited directly from HS/club with no prior college, per the live 2025 goduke.com roster. 4 transferred in — from Georgetown, Harvard, College of Charleston, and Northeastern.
minutesOutlook.trajectoryNote: 7 of 15 cleared, but 6 Fr remain as Jrs competing directly with Olivier through Yr1-2. Duke recruits elite midfielders every cycle — squad competition is very high. Roster count verified by browser; names not captured.

name: NC State
kinRank: Good (Exercise and Sport Science, ACC-level sports science resources)
acuAlignNote: Solid exercise science — anatomy, physiology, biomechanics, and motor control covered. Pre-PT pathway available through kinesiology advising at NC State. Raleigh's growing healthcare sector (WakeMed, UNC Health) provides clinical placement access.

fin.internationalNote: NC State out-of-state COA is ~$52k/yr (2025-26). Athletic scholarship can cover 35–55% of COA. Merit aid available for international students with 3.0+ GPA. Raleigh cost of living is moderate. Research Triangle location with strong career pathways.

gpa.note: Strong public university admission. International athletes typically on full athletic scholarship.

confRecord.note: 4-1-2 ACC (14 pts, 16-2-3 overall). 3rd in the ACC; lost the ACC Tournament quarterfinal to Syracuse 0-3, then reached the NCAA Championship final (national runners-up). Verified 2026 via theacc.com official standings.
3-3-2 ACC (11 pts, 10-5-5). 9th in the single-table ACC.
1-5-2 ACC (5 pts, 6-9-3). 5th in the ACC Atlantic Division.
1-5-2 ACC (5 pts, 6-7-5). Last in the ACC Atlantic Division.
1-5-2 ACC (5 pts, 7-8-2). Last in the ACC Atlantic Division.
1-2-3 ACC (6 pts, 3-8-4). 4th in the ACC Atlantic Division (2020-21 COVID season).

proPlayers.draftRank: Developing MLS pipeline in the ACC era

devScoresNote: v42.26 §5a re-baseline (training environment only). Live gopack.com MSOC staff: HC Marc Hubbard + 3 Asst (Augusto Lima, Jon Shaad, Jordan Valentine) = 4 full-time coaches (no GK) + Director of Operations, plus shared university services listed on the page (ATC, an Assistant S&C coach, Olympic-sports nutrition, sport psychologist — all shared, common to every D1). Stored Tier-1 facilities: Dail Soccer Field/Track Complex 3,000 shared with track (not soccer-specific), Karr Performance shared. Mid-tier ACC environment: shared everything and a shared-track stadium. Essentially held at 73/72/74.

coach.name: Marc Hubbard
coach.title: Head Coach
coach.profile: Marc Hubbard, the 10th head coach in NC State history, took the Wolfpack to the program's first-ever national championship game in 2025 (first Final Four in 35 years) after a 2024 Sweet Sixteen — in just two seasons. He developed the 2025 MAC Hermann Trophy winner (Donavan Phillip) and NC State's first-ever No. 1 overall MLS SuperDraft pick (Nikola Markovic, 2026). Raleigh's rapidly growing youth-soccer market provides strong regional recruiting.

courses: ESS 301 — Anatomy and Physiology of Exercise
rec: Best value ACC option in a genuinely exciting city. Raleigh is one of the fastest-growing cities in the USA — affordable, warm-adjacent, vibrant. GPA 2.8 aligns with admission. Realistic minutes path as a mid-tier ACC program.

culture.vibe: Large public research university (37,000 students) with a STEM and engineering identity — NC State is the tech powerhouse of the Research Triangle. Raleigh is one of the USA's fastest-growing cities, with a genuine food scene, pro sports, and creative culture. Campus is collaborative and entrepreneurial rather than elite or traditional.
culture.thingsToDo: Raleigh's Glenwood South restaurant and bar strip, NC Museum of Art (free), Carolina Hurricanes NHL hockey, NC FC soccer, Umstead State Park cycling and hiking, American Tobacco Trail. Chapel Hill 30 min, Durham 20 min, beaches 2.5hrs.
culture.socialScene: Large campus with high energy around football and basketball but genuine soccer culture too. International students well-represented especially in engineering and science programs. Wolfpack pride is infectious. Greek life present but not dominant.
culture.olivierMatch: ★★★★ Raleigh is a genuinely excellent lifestyle match — warm-adjacent, growing, affordable, vibrant. GPA 2.8 fits admission. Lower cost makes this one of the best value ACC options. Mid-tier ACC competition means more realistic playing minutes.
culture.lifestyleTags: Growing Sunbelt city, tech hub, affordable, vibrant

facilityDetails.stadium: Dail Soccer Field/Track Complex — 3,000 capacity, shared with track, on-campus, lights. Opened 2008.
facilityDetails.trainingFields: Practice facilities adjacent to Dail Stadium with access to Carter-Finley precinct training fields. Synthetic turf training surface available for year-round conditioning work.
facilityDetails.strengthConditioning: Karr Athletics Performance Facility — shared Olympic sports weight room. Free weights, Olympic platforms, Keiser strength equipment. Sports dietitian and sport psychologist available through the athletics department.
facilityDetails.sportsScience: GPS monitoring (Polar Team Pro) for match and training tracking. Hudl video analysis system. NC State's Exercise and Sport Science department labs available for athlete physiological testing.
facilityDetails.sportsMed: NC State Athletics Sports Medicine — dedicated athletic trainer for soccer program. WakeMed and UNC Health access for specialist orthopaedics. Solid sports medicine coverage appropriate for ACC competition.
facilityDetails.academicLabs: Dept of Exercise and Sport Science: anatomy and physiology teaching labs, biomechanics lab, fitness assessment suite, sport and exercise psychology lab. NC State's STEM focus supports pre-PT pathway research.
facilityDetails.extras: Carter-Finley Stadium (57,500 cap), PNC Arena (Hurricanes NHL), Wolfpack Sports complex. Raleigh's city amenities — pro sports, restaurants, cycling trails — extend the campus experience into a dynamic urban environment.
facilityDetails.note: Wolfpack Training Complex adjacent: grass field, turf field, lights, dedicated locker rooms and sports medicine facility. Solid ACC support infrastructure despite shared use with track.

housing.note: Mandatory for first-year students; exemptions limited to disability/medical need, religious requirements, or living with parents within 15 miles. Verified v38 via official housing.dasa.ncsu.edu.

minutesOutlook.recruit_pathway_note: 7 of 12 current MFs (58%) recruited directly from HS/club with no prior college, per the live 2025 gopack.com roster; 5 transferred in, including two multi-stop JUCO pathways (Carlos Santamaria: Iowa Western CC→Oral Roberts→Mercer; Thomas Lamaille: Kansas City Kansas CC→Tyler JC→St. John's) plus Georgia State, New Hampshire, and Missouri State. Consistent with new HC Marc Hubbard's rapid 2-season rebuild relying heavily on the transfer portal.
minutesOutlook.trajectoryNote: 5 of 7 MFs cleared — near-complete midfield rebuild. Only 2 remain by 2027. Heavy recruiting expected. Roster count verified by browser MCP; names not captured.

name: Louisville
kinRank: Good (Exercise Science, ties to UofL health sciences campus)

coach.title: Head Coach
coach.profile: Developing Louisville's presence in the ACC with a strong focus on international recruiting. Hayden's teams are known for energetic, pressing-oriented soccer. The program is actively rebuilding its identity in the ACC — a window of opportunity for an international CM who could make an impact quickly.

facilities: Dr. Mark & Cindy Lynn Stadium (3,000 cap)
rec: Best value in the ACC for Olivier. Warm mid-sized city with genuine character. GPA 2.5 entry bar means admission is realistic. UofL Health gives rare pre-PT clinical access. A rebuilding program means early playing time is more achievable.

culture.vibe: Louisville is a Midwest-South hybrid city with genuine warmth — think smaller-scale Nashville meets Ohio Valley character. UofL campus is urban and accessible, embedded in the city. The NuLu arts district, bourbon country proximity, and a growing food and tech scene make Louisville more vibrant than its reputation suggests.
culture.thingsToDo: NuLu Arts District restaurants and galleries, Louisville Slugger Museum, Churchill Downs (Kentucky Derby), bourbon distillery tours, Big Four Bridge over the Ohio River, Louisville Zoo. Cincinnati 1.5hrs, Nashville 2.5hrs. Louisville City FC (USL Championship) for local soccer culture.
culture.socialScene: Mid-sized urban university campus (23,000 students). Cardinal pride is strong across all sports. International student community present in engineering and health sciences. Louisville City FC connection gives a professional soccer reference point. Growing music and arts scene.
culture.olivierMatch: ★★★★ Louisville is one of Olivier's best value-for-money ACC options — GPA fits, city feel is real, cost is the lowest in the ACC at ~$45k, and UofL Health provides genuine DPT clinical access. A rebuilding program means more realistic early playing time.
culture.lifestyleTags: Urban campus, warm-adjacent city, good value, real city character

facilityDetails.stadium: Dr. Mark & Cindy Lynn Stadium — 5,300 capacity (2,300 chairback + 900 bleacher + 2,100 berm), dedicated soccer facility opened 2014. $18.5M construction. FieldTurf surface.
facilityDetails.trainingFields: Practice sessions at on-campus training surfaces and nearby facilities. Indoor facility access through the ShelbyHurst Campus recreation complex for winter conditioning.
facilityDetails.strengthConditioning: ShelbyHurst Athletic Facility and Kueber Strength Center — recently upgraded ACC-standard weight rooms. Soccer team S&C programming supported by professional coaches. Sports nutrition counselling available through athletic department.
facilityDetails.sportsScience: GPS monitoring and Hudl video analysis in use. Louisville's Exercise Science program provides access to physiology testing and sport science labs for student-athletes seeking advanced monitoring.
facilityDetails.sportsMed: UofL Athletics Sports Medicine — certified ATCs for soccer. UofL Health is one of the largest regional medical systems in the South with Level 1 Trauma and a dedicated sports medicine clinic. Orthopaedic and surgical access is among the best at any public ACC school.
facilityDetails.academicLabs: Department of Exercise Science: biomechanics lab, exercise physiology testing suites, anatomy cadaver lab access. UofL Health's DPT program creates genuine clinical placement opportunities for pre-PT students — a real differentiator at this cost point.
facilityDetails.extras: KFC Yum! Center (Louisville basketball arena), Cardinal Stadium (football). Louisville City FC connection for soccer culture. NuLu creative district gives the campus urban creative energy atypical of comparable Midwest schools.
facilityDetails.note: 15,320 sqft on-site training centre: dedicated locker rooms, sports medicine room, 830 sqft video/meeting room, indoor warm-up area. One of the best mid-major soccer facility packages in the ACC.

housing.note: Mandatory first-year live-on requirement; automatic exemptions only for local KY/IN counties or age 21+. Verified v38 via official louisville.edu/housing.

minutesOutlook.recruit_pathway_note: 8 of 9 current MFs (89%) recruited directly from HS/club with no prior college, per the live 2025 gocards.com roster — only Bilal Camara transferred in (Limestone, D2).
minutesOutlook.trajectoryNote: 6 of 9 MFs cleared before 2027. Strong opportunity for an ACC program — 2 rising juniors provide peer competition. Roster count verified; names not captured.

name: Pittsburgh
kinRank: Good (Exercise Science, UPMC sports medicine partnerships nearby)
acuAlignNote: Exercise Science covers anatomy, physiology, biomechanics, motor control, and sport psychology. UPMC partnership gives Pitt athletes access to world-class sports medicine infrastructure — one of the top 5 sports medicine systems globally. Pre-PT pathway is among the strongest in this guide.

fin.internationalNote: Pitt out-of-state COA is ~$58k/yr (2025-26). Athletic scholarship can cover 30–50% of COA. Merit aid available for international students. Pittsburgh has affordable urban cost of living compared to eastern coastal cities. Strong health sciences pathway.

gpa.note: Solid public university. International athletes supported via athletic department.

confRecord.note: Beat Clemson R1 1-0, lost QF to Cal 0-1.

proPlayers.draftRank: Developing pipeline with upward trajectory under Jay Vidovich

devScoresNote: v42.26 §5a re-baseline; LIVE-VERIFIED v42.33 via pittsburghpanthers.com (the working host — the stored pittPanthers.com is a dead alias that blanks, which is why v42.26 scored it from stored data). Live MSOC staff = HC Jay Vidovich + 3 assistants (Cregan, Marshall, Dambrot): a deep 4-coach staff, but NO soccer-dedicated S&C or sports-science staffer listed. HC pedigree elite (2007 Wake Forest national title; results excluded). Live check confirms the conservative 75/73/74 — unchanged.

coach.name: Jay Vidovich
coach.title: Head Coach
coach.profile: Experienced ACC coach who led Wake Forest to the 2007 NCAA Championship. At Pitt since ~2022, immediately establishing the program as an ACC contender — 2024 ACC Regular Season Champions in his tenure. Known for possession-based, technically refined soccer. Former Wake Forest success shows his ability to build elite programs at private ACC schools.

rec: Pittsburgh punches above its weight — a genuine city with one of the best sports medicine environments in the world via UPMC. 2024 ACC Regular Season Champions shows the soccer quality. Cold winters are a real lifestyle challenge for Olivier. UPMC's pre-PT access is unmatched.

culture.vibe: Pittsburgh is a genuinely great city that has undergone a remarkable renaissance — the 'most liveable city' moniker is earned. The university sits in Oakland, a vibrant student-and-medical district shared by Carnegie Mellon, UPMC, and Pitt. The city is architecturally beautiful, walkable, multicultural, and passionate about its sports (Steelers, Pirates, Penguins).
culture.thingsToDo: Strip District food market, Primanti Brothers sandwiches, Carnegie Museums of Art and Natural History, Duquesne and Monongahela Inclines for city views, Three Rivers trail system, South Side nightlife, PNC Park river setting. Allegheny National Forest hiking 1.5hrs, Seven Springs skiing 1hr.
culture.socialScene: Pitt has genuine football culture plus strong basketball. Soccer team culture is building rapidly with Vidovich's recent success. Oakland student district blends university energy, medical professionals, and startup culture. Strong international student presence in engineering and health sciences.
culture.olivierMatch: ★★★ Pittsburgh is a real city with one of the best sports medicine environments in the world. The UPMC access for pre-PT is the strongest argument for any city-campus school in this guide. Cold winters (the coldest of any city-campus option) are the main lifestyle trade-off.
culture.lifestyleTags: Real city, UPMC access, cold winters, historic industrial character

facilityDetails.stadium: Ambrose Urbanic Field at Petersen Sports Complex — 735 capacity, FieldTurf, lights, press box. Shared with baseball, softball and lacrosse. Opened 2011.
facilityDetails.trainingFields: Additional practice field adjacent to Ambrose Urbanic. Access to Pitt's Pete Peterson Sports Complex — 50-acre athletics facility with multiple training surfaces.
facilityDetails.strengthConditioning: Trees Hall and Pitt strength and conditioning centre — fully upgraded 2021. Olympic lifting platforms, Keiser equipment, dedicated team pods. Soccer-specific S&C programming. Recovery tech: Normatec, ice baths, sports nutrition guidance.
facilityDetails.sportsScience: GPS tracking (Catapult) for all sessions. Hudl and XPS video analysis. Pitt's exercise science labs provide VO2max testing, lactate profiling, and force plate analysis — sophisticated for a mid-tier ACC program.
facilityDetails.sportsMed: UPMC — University of Pittsburgh Medical Center — is one of the top 5 sports medicine systems in the world. Olympic and professional athletes travel to UPMC for orthopaedic procedures. Pitt student-athletes access UPMC orthopaedics, sports science, and rehabilitation that most D1 programs can only read about.
facilityDetails.academicLabs: Pitt's Dept of Exercise Science in the Dietrich School: anatomy lab with full cadaver access, biomechanics motion capture lab, exercise physiology suites, motor control lab, sport psychology training rooms. Pre-PT advising is integrated — Pitt runs a top-15 DPT program.
facilityDetails.extras: Heinz Field (Pittsburgh Steelers) 2km, PNC Park (Pirates), PPG Paints Arena (Penguins). Pittsburgh's unique hillside geography and three-river setting are unlike any other college city in the guide.
facilityDetails.note: 3rd floor added 2020 with video room, team lounge and coaches suites. Pitt main athletic S&C facilities accessible across campus. Compact urban campus facility — solid but limited by shared use and small capacity.

housing.note: Guaranteed (not strictly mandatory) for qualifying first-year fall-term students who meet application/deposit deadlines; guarantee extends to 2 years for incoming classes from Fall 2026. Verified v38 via official pc.pitt.edu (Panther Central).

minutesOutlook.recruit_pathway_note: 9 of 10 current MFs (90%) recruited directly from HS/club with no prior college, per the live 2025 pittsburghpanthers.com roster — only Tomas Bedouret transferred in (Oregon State).
minutesOutlook.trajectoryNote: 4 of 5 MFs cleared — near-complete rebuild. Only 1 So remains as Sr in Yr1. Pitt will recruit heavily to fill midfield depth. Roster count verified; names not captured.

name: Stanford
kinRank: Excellent (Human Biology — highly selective interdisciplinary pre-health major)
acuAlignNote: Exceptional — Stanford's Human Biology major covers anatomy, physiology, biomechanics, neuroscience, and motor control at research depth. 13 of 16 ACU units have direct equivalents. Stanford Medical Center's DPT program is top-5 nationally — pre-PT students can access clinical hours and mentorship unavailable anywhere else in the guide.

fin.internationalNote: Stanford full COA is ~$88k/yr (2025-26). Athletic scholarship + need-based aid can combine to substantially reduce costs — Stanford runs one of the most generous need-based aid programs in the US. Bay Area living costs are high but packages reflect this. Highly selective.

gpa.note: Extremely selective. Most competitive admission in the guide. Athletic aid essential.

confRecord.note: 5-2-1 ACC (16 pts, 14-4-2 overall). 2nd seed; beat Boston College in the ACC Tournament first round, lost the quarterfinal to SMU 0-1. Verified 2026 via theacc.com official standings.
3-2-3 ACC (12 pts, 9-5-6). 7th in the ACC in its first season after moving from the Pac-12.
7-2-1 Pac-12 (10-3-1 overall). Pac-12 regular-season champions (2020-21 season, played in spring 2021).

proPlayers.draftRank: Top 10 all-time MLS producers — consistent pipeline to professional soccer

devScoresNote: v42.26 §5a re-baseline (training environment only). Live gostanford.com MSOC staff: HC Jeremy Gunn (endowed Knowles Family Director of Men's Soccer) + Drew Hutchins (Assoc HC) + Woo Jeon (Asst) + Kevin McCarthy (Asst) = 4 full-time coaches. Stored Tier-1 facilities: Maloney Field at Cagan Stadium, dedicated natural-grass soccer venue used by US national teams and MLS clubs for training (elite pitch), 3 dedicated training pitches, Arrillaga indoor, world-class but shared (not soccer-dedicated) sports science, year-round California climate. coach.name: Jeremy Gunn
coach.title: Head Coach
coach.profile: Former US U-20 National Team Head Coach. Former Colorado Rapids assistant. At Stanford since ~2019. The Stanford soccer environment is one of the most elite development systems in college soccer — attracting players capable of professional careers. Gunn's background at national team level means his player development framework is world-class.

courses: PSYC 256 — Psychology of Sport and Exercise
rec: The ceiling school of this guide — best academic and soccer combination available anywhere. Bay Area lifestyle is the closest match to Sydney in the entire USA. GPA 2.8 is well below Stanford's 3.9+ floor. The athletic pathway is the only realistic route and requires an exceptional international profile.

culture.vibe: Stanford is 8,000 acres of California sunshine, Spanish Mission architecture, and world-class intellect. Palo Alto and the surrounding Bay Area tech corridor (Google, Apple, Meta all within 20km) creates a unique environment combining elite academics, entrepreneurship, and California lifestyle. The student body is exceptional — and expects no less from itself.
culture.thingsToDo: San Francisco 45 min by Caltrain, Santa Cruz beaches 1hr, Big Sur coast drive 2hrs, Yosemite 3hrs, Napa Valley wineries 1hr, Tahoe skiing 3hrs. Stanford campus itself — Dish hike, Palm Drive, Lake Lagunita — is genuinely beautiful for outdoor recreation.
culture.socialScene: Stanford undergrad community is small (7,000) and fiercely accomplished. Student-athletes make up ~20% of undergrads — athletics is woven into the culture here in a way it isn't at most elite academic schools. International student community is celebrated. Soccer program alumni culture is strong.
culture.olivierMatch: ★★ The Bay Area is the lifestyle match of Olivier's dreams — California sun, major city 45 min away, beach culture. But Stanford's admission is near-impossible without a 3.9+ GPA and elite-level athletic profile. The dream school that requires a different academic trajectory to reach.
culture.lifestyleTags: California sunshine, Bay Area tech culture, elite prestige, warm climate

facilityDetails.stadium: Maloney Field at Laird Q. Cagan Stadium — 2,000 capacity, natural grass, lights. Dedicated soccer use (shared with lacrosse). Used by US national teams and MLS clubs for training.
facilityDetails.trainingFields: Three dedicated training pitches at the Cagan Stadium precinct, all natural grass. Indoor training space in Arrillaga Family Sports Center. California's climate means outdoor training is viable 11 months per year.
facilityDetails.strengthConditioning: Stanford Athletics' Arrillaga Family Sports Center — world-class performance facility. Sports performance staff are research-affiliated — publishing in top journals while coaching. Soccer has dedicated S&C programming reflecting Stanford's elite expectations.
facilityDetails.sportsScience: Stanford is at the frontier of applied sport science. GPS (Catapult Vector), force plate analysis (VALD), HRV biometrics, motion capture, and VO2/lactate testing via Stanford's Exercise & Wellness labs. Multiple coaching staff hold or are completing PhD-level qualifications.
facilityDetails.sportsMed: Stanford Sports Medicine is a research institution, not just a treatment service. Stanford Medical Center (top-5 hospital globally) means student-athletes access cutting-edge sports injury management, return-to-play research, and concussion protocols that other programs read about.
facilityDetails.academicLabs: Stanford's Human Biology major accesses research labs across multiple schools — neuroscience labs in Beckman Center, physiology labs in Clark Center, biomechanics in the Neuromuscular Biomechanics Lab. Pre-PT shadowing at Stanford Health Care's outpatient DPT clinics is available.
facilityDetails.extras: Stanford's 8,000-acre campus includes the Hoover Institution, SLAC National Accelerator, engineering quad, and multiple world-class sports facilities (swimming, tennis, football). Apple Park, Googleplex, and Meta HQ are 20-30 minutes away.
facilityDetails.note: Modest stadium capacity but world-class Stanford athletic ecosystem — elite S&C, Rathbun Training Center sports medicine, performance science. Natural grass surface preferred by national teams. Stanford program prestige offsets small stadium.

housing.note: Mandatory for first-year students (explicit residency requirement); guaranteed housing for 12 quarters across the degree. Verified v38 via official rde.stanford.edu.

minutesOutlook.recruit_pathway_note: Live 2026 gostanford.com roster template does not publish prior-school data for any player (unlike most other schools' rosters), so individual transfer status could not be directly verified. Classified Freshman-friendly on structural grounds: Stanford's own undergraduate admissions (3.7+ GPA, 13% acceptance) are separate from athletics and apply equally to transfers, making transfer-in unusually rare compared to any other school in this guide. Lower confidence than other entries — flag for re-verification if a more detailed source is found.
minutesOutlook.trajectoryNote: Only 2 MFs clear (Kahoalii Jr, Islam R-Jr). 4 So compete as Srs in Yr1 — midfield is congested. Stanford's elite recruiting cycle maintains perpetual competition.

name: Syracuse
acuAlignNote: Exercise Science through Falk College covers anatomy, physiology, biomechanics, motor control, and sport psychology. Pre-PT pathway available through kinesiology advising. Upstate Medical University affiliation gives clinical access above typical D1 standard.

fin.internationalNote: Syracuse full COA is ~$78k/yr (2025-26). Athletic scholarship can cover 25–45% of COA. Merit aid available for strong academic records. Upstate NY has moderate cost of living. Strong international student community and sports management programs.

gpa.note: Private university — merit aid available alongside athletic scholarship.

confRecord.note: 6th seed. Beat Va Tech R1, beat NC State QF, lost SF to SMU 1-5.

proPlayers.draftRank: Growing pipeline — 2022 national championship boosted program visibility

devScoresNote: v42.26 §5a re-baseline (training environment only). Live cuse.com MSOC staff: HC Ian McIntyre + Jukka Masalin (Assoc HC) + Matty Evans (Asst) = 3 full-time coaches only, no GK coach, no sports-science listed. Stored Tier-1 facilities: SU Soccer Stadium 2,000 dedicated natural grass, shared adjacent facilities, indoor via the basketball complex. §5a's own diagnosis names Syracuse's old 78 as title-inflated ('Indian Hills tying Syracuse at 78'); the 2022 national title is a result and excluded. Environment-only: thin 3-coach staff, modest dedicated stadium, everything shared. McIntyre's pedigree props tactical. Trimmed from 80/78/76 to 76/74/73.

coach.name: Ian McIntyre
coach.title: Head Coach
coach.profile: Led Syracuse to the 2022 NCAA Championship — one of the most significant titles in program history. Building Syracuse into a consistent ACC contender with a technically organised, pressing-based system. McIntyre's coaching environment produces disciplined, tactically aware midfielders. The 2022 title demonstrates the program's ceiling.

rec: The 2022 NCAA Championship proves the soccer quality. Expensive private university in a very cold city — Olivier's most significant lifestyle trade-off. Falk College's sport management and exercise science reputation is genuine. For a player who can handle upstate NY winters, the soccer quality is unquestionable.

culture.vibe: Syracuse is a passionate college sports city built around the university. 'Cuse' is the entire identity of this small upstate New York city — Orange pride is all-pervasive. Falk College of Sport and Human Dynamics is one of the best in the USA. Campus is architecturally eclectic, set on a hill. The climate is genuinely punishing — one of the snowiest cities in the continental USA.
culture.thingsToDo: Armory Square dining and nightlife, Destiny USA (largest mall in New York State), Syracuse Stage theatre, Onondaga Lake Park, Turning Stone Resort 40 min, Finger Lakes wine region 45 min. NYC 4.5hrs direct train. Skiing at Greek Peak or Song Mountain 45 min.
culture.socialScene: Orange sports culture is intense — the dome (JMA Wireless Arena) seats 35,000 and creates remarkable atmosphere. Soccer program national championship reputation since 2022. Greek life active. International students present but in a minority.
culture.olivierMatch: ★★ The 2022 NCAA Championship shows the soccer quality. But Syracuse is cold — very cold, regularly among the snowiest US cities. The city is small. For Olivier, the lifestyle is a genuine sacrifice. Strong value for players who can look past the climate.
culture.lifestyleTags: College sports town, NCAA champions, very cold winters, small city

facilityDetails.stadium: SU Soccer Stadium — 2,000 capacity, on-campus natural grass facility with lights. Compact and atmospherically charged by passionate Orange fans. Surface and upkeep well-maintained for ACC play.
facilityDetails.trainingFields: Practice facilities adjacent to the main stadium plus access to Ensley Athletic Complex. Indoor training space available in the Carmelo K. Anthony Basketball Center precinct — essential given Syracuse's climate (heavy snow Oct–April).
facilityDetails.strengthConditioning: Manley Field House strength and conditioning centre — shared Olympic sports facility. Free weights, Olympic platforms, Keiser equipment. Sports performance staff and sports dietitian available.
facilityDetails.sportsScience: GPS tracking (Catapult) and Hudl video analysis in use. Falk College's exercise science labs provide physiological testing — VO2, biomechanics, sport psychology for student-athletes.
facilityDetails.sportsMed: Upstate Medical University (SUNY) affiliation gives student-athletes access to specialist sports medicine above typical D1 standards. Full-time ATCs on staff and team physician for game days.
facilityDetails.academicLabs: Falk College of Sport and Human Dynamics: Exercise Science major includes biomechanics lab, anatomy/physiology labs, sport performance testing centre. Falk's sport management facilities are a unique additional asset for future career pathways.
facilityDetails.extras: JMA Wireless Dome (35,000+ capacity) — one of the most recognisable venues in college sport. Syracuse's sport management legacy (ESPN, Nike executives trace career roots here). New York City 4.5hrs direct train.
facilityDetails.note: The 2022 NCAA Championship validates program quality. The SU Soccer Stadium's intimate atmosphere and Falk College's academic reputation make this a well-rounded D1 program — climate is the single Olivier-specific concern.

housing.note: Mandatory for first- and second-year students (2 years), with a commuting exemption only if immediate family lives in the Syracuse area. Verified v38 via official housingmealplans.syr.edu.

minutesOutlook.recruit_pathway_note: 7 of 10 current MFs (70%) recruited directly from HS/club with no prior college, per the live 2025 cuse.com roster. 3 transferred in — Nathan Scott (UC Riverside), Carlos Zambrano (Daytona State, JUCO), Tim Noeding (Bethel, NAIA).
minutesOutlook.trajectoryNote: Only 1 MF clears before 2027. 4 So and 5 Fr remain — midfield extremely crowded in Yr1-2. Patience required; Yr3 breakthrough as seniors clear. Roster count verified; names not captured.

acuAlignNote: Excellent — Integrative Human Physiology covers anatomy, physiology, motor control, biomechanics, neuroscience. Berkeley's research depth is world-class. 12 of 16 ACU units have direct equivalents. UCSF Health connection provides exceptional pre-PT clinical placement access.

gpa.note: Highly selective UC campus. International athletes qualify for athletic aid.

2-4-2 ACC (8 pts, 8-8-2). 12th in the ACC in its first season after moving from the Pac-12.

1-6-1 Pac-12 (3-9-3 overall). Last in the Pac-12.

devScoresNote: v42.26 §5a re-baseline (training environment only). Live calbears.com MSOC staff: HC Leonard Griffin + 3 Asst (Ricardo Gutierrez, Jacob Wilson, Henry Foulk) = 4 full-time coaches (no GK) + Philippe Dupont (High Performance Coordinator, soccer-attached) + Asst Recruiting Coordinator. Stored Tier-1 facilities: Goldman Field at Edwards Stadium 3,500 natural grass, renovated 2019 (unique historic setting), 2 training pitches, shared RSF indoor. (Note: live HC is Leonard Griffin vs stored Colin Carmichael — a separate Change Type 2 to reconcile.)

coach.name: Leonard Griffin
coach.title: Head Coach
coach.profile: Leonard Griffin, the 2023 Pac-12 Coach of the Year, has led Cal since 2022, guiding the program's transition from the Pac-12 into the ACC. A UCLA national champion as a player (2002) and former MLS pro (Chicago Fire, LA Galaxy), Griffin has developed 35+ players into the professional ranks across stops at Cal, UCLA and Portland and as head coach at Grand Canyon (2021 WAC title, NCAA berth) and San Francisco. His Bay Area recruiting network draws from one of the most soccer-rich regions in the USA; the 2025 ACC Tournament semi-final run showed Cal can compete with the ACC elite.

facilities: Goldman Field at Edwards Stadium (3,500 cap)
rec: Bay Area lifestyle is the closest match to Sydney's urban-coastal character in the entire guide. Warm California climate, Oakland/SF access, strong research physiology path for DPT. GPA 2.8 is below Cal's 3.0+ floor — athletic pathway is the only realistic route, and it exists.

culture.vibe: UC Berkeley is a world institution whose scale, diversity, and intellectual ambition are unmatched in this guide. The Bay Area surrounding it — Oakland directly adjacent, San Francisco 30 min across the Bay — provides a lifestyle so rich it's almost unfair. Berkeley's intellectual culture leans research-heavy, globally minded, and socially diverse.
culture.thingsToDo: San Francisco 30 min by BART from campus, Oakland food scene (Temescal, Uptown), Berkeley's Telegraph Ave and restaurant scene, Tilden Regional Park hiking, Berkeley Marina, Napa Valley wineries 1hr, Tahoe skiing 2hrs, Big Sur 2.5hrs. San Jose Earthquakes MLS 45 min.
culture.socialScene: 43,000-student university with enormous cultural diversity — one of the most globally diverse campuses in the USA. Soccer program transitioning from Pac-12 success to ACC competition and still building its ACC identity. Bay Area's concentration of international communities makes this among the most globally welcoming environments in the guide.
culture.olivierMatch: ★★★★ Bay Area lifestyle is the closest match to Sydney's coastal-urban character in the entire guide. Warm California climate, city access, beach proximity, global diversity. GPA 2.8 is below Cal's 3.0 floor — the athletic pathway is the only way in, and it exists.
culture.lifestyleTags: Bay Area, California lifestyle, global diversity, world-class research

facilityDetails.stadium: Goldman Field at Edwards Stadium — 3,500 capacity, natural grass surface, lights, renovated 2019. Sits inside the historic stone Edwards Stadium architecture — a unique setting unlike any other college soccer venue.
facilityDetails.trainingFields: Two training pitches adjacent to Goldman Field. Access to Memorial Stadium precinct for conditioning work. Indoor training in the RSF (Recreational Sports Facility — 100,000 sq ft, one of the largest university recreation facilities in the USA).
facilityDetails.strengthConditioning: RSF and Haas Pavilion strength and conditioning facilities. 20,000 sq ft weight room, Olympic platforms, Keiser equipment. Research-informed S&C approach through Cal's kinesiology and public health faculties.
facilityDetails.sportsScience: GPS tracking (Catapult), Hudl video analysis. Cal's Integrative Human Physiology research labs can be accessed by student-athletes with faculty relationships. Sports science integration growing as Cal adjusts to ACC competition standards.
facilityDetails.sportsMed: Cal's sports medicine department is connected to UCSF (UC San Francisco) Health — one of the top medical schools in the world. Access to orthopaedic specialists and sports medicine physicians via UCSF partnership. Full-time ATCs on soccer roster.
facilityDetails.academicLabs: Division of Biological Sciences and Public Health: physiology and human movement labs, biomechanics research lab in Movement Sciences. Pre-PT pathways supported through UCSF's DPT program connections — one of the top 10 DPT programs nationally.
facilityDetails.extras: Cal Memorial Stadium (63,000 cap), Haas Pavilion basketball arena, the RSF complex. Berkeley's location makes the entire Bay Area essentially an extension of campus — BART from campus to downtown SF in 30 min.
facilityDetails.note: Goldman Field's setting inside historic Edwards Stadium is architecturally distinctive. The Bay Area location and UCSF clinical connection make Cal one of the most lifestyle-positive and clinically-advantaged options in the entire guide for a pre-PT student.

housing.note: Not mandatory, but incoming first-years (2026-27) are guaranteed a housing offer for their first 2 years if deadlines are met; international students also have access to the dedicated International House community. Verified v38 via official housing.berkeley.edu.

acRank: Strong — B.S. Health Science: Preprofessional Health Studies
kinRank: B.S. Health Science — explicit pre-professional health pathway incl. physical therapy
degreeTitle: B.S. Health Science: Preprofessional Health Studies
acuAlignNote: Partial alignment — Clemson's Health Science: Preprofessional degree is a public-health pathway, not exercise science. Required courses cover anatomy (BIOL 2220/2230), human biology, statistics, research methods, and a health internship — but there is no exercise physiology, no biomechanics, no resistance training, no sport psychology, and no motor control in the required curriculum. Strong for PT school admission prerequisites (MUSC DPT pathway is a genuine clinical advantage), but structurally different from ACU BESS.
soccerLevel: ACC Conference — D1 Elite. 2× recent NCAA Champions.
prePT: Good — pre-health public pathway covers PT school prerequisites. Degree is not exercise-science based. MUSC DPT pathway is the main clinical advantage.

gpa.note: GPA 2.8 is a challenge for Clemson admission — athletic scholarship is the key lever. Clemson is selective (53% acceptance). Pre-PT degree requires maintained GPA for progression.

fin.internationalNote: Out-of-state tuition ~$38k. Athletic scholarship can cover majority. Clemson has strong international student support. Merit aid available for high academic performers.

culture.vibe: Large, spirited Southern university with intense athletic culture. Clemson is famous for its football program but soccer carries real prestige — 2× recent national champions. Campus is in a small college town (Clemson, SC) but Atlanta is 2hrs away.
culture.olivierMatch: ★★★ Clemson is warm (South Carolina climate), has elite soccer development, and strong pre-PT degree. The campus town is small — not a city. Athletic culture is intense and competitive. If Olivier can handle a non-city environment, the soccer development opportunity is exceptional.
culture.socialScene: Huge college-town social scene driven by athletics. International players on soccer roster. Tight-knit team environment — Noonan known for player development culture.
culture.thingsToDo: Clemson is a small town — social life is campus-centred. Lake Hartwell (boating/kayaking) adjacent to campus. Greenville SC (45 min) is a vibrant city with excellent food scene. Atlanta, GA (2hr) for big-city weekends. Blue Ridge Mountains hiking 1hr. Charlotte NC 2hrs.
culture.lifestyleTags: Warm climate, small college town, elite soccer culture

facilityDetails.stadium: Historic Riggs Field — 8,000 capacity, one of the most famous college soccer venues in America. Natural grass. On-campus.
facilityDetails.trainingFields: Multiple practice fields adjacent to Riggs Field. Climate allows year-round outdoor training in South Carolina.
facilityDetails.strengthConditioning: Clemson Athletics world-class S&C — Allen N. Reeves Football Complex shared resources. Olympic lifting platforms, Hammer Strength, GPS and sports science integration.
facilityDetails.sportsScience: Full GPS tracking (Catapult), video analysis (Hudl + XOS), heart rate monitoring, lactate testing. One of the most advanced S&C setups in college soccer.
facilityDetails.academicLabs: Watt Family Innovation Center + Health Sciences facilities. Preprofessional Health Studies degree has dedicated labs. MUSC partnership creates access to world-class medical school facilities for clinical placements.
facilityDetails.sportsMed: Full-time certified AT staff, team physician, sports psychology support. Recovery facilities — hydrotherapy, cryotherapy, altitude simulation. MUSC accelerated DPT pathway gives direct clinical exposure.
facilityDetails.extras: Riggs Field atmosphere on gameday is elite. Lake Hartwell adjacent — boating, kayaking. Greenville, SC (45min) is ranked one of the best mid-size cities in the Southeast. Atlanta access. Blue Ridge hiking.
facilityDetails.note: Clemson offers one of the best overall packages in college soccer — elite facilities, elite coaching, elite degree for PT pathway, warm climate. The trade-off is a small-town setting and high competition for playing time (4 players drafted in first round in 2026 alone).

housing.note: Mandatory and guaranteed for all first-year/Bridge students; exemption only for living with parents in the 3 counties surrounding campus (Anderson, Pickens, Oconee). Verified v38 via official housing.clemson.edu.

coach.title: Head Men's Soccer Coach

Known for technical development and high-press tactical style. Staff: Philip Jones (13yr Assoc HC), Camilo Rodriguez (10yr Asst), Gaye Diadie (Asst).

Soccer) = 4 full-time coaches + analytics + a soccer-dedicated athletic trainer. HLTH 4900 — Research & Evaluation Strategies for Public Health
rec: Clemson is elite-tier ACC soccer with 2 recent national championships and the best MLS draft pipeline of the last 2 years. The Pre-PT degree is excellent — explicitly designed for physical therapy and medical school pathways with access to MUSC's DPT program. South Carolina climate is warm. The challenge: Clemson is a small college town (not a city), competition for playing time is fierce (4 first-rounders in 2026), and GPA 2.8 is borderline for admission. If Olivier can secure an athletic scholarship offer, this is among the top 3 programs in the entire guide for soccer development.

minutesOutlook.recruit_pathway_note: Live 2025-26 clemsontigers.com roster template does not publish HS/prior-school data for any player, so individual transfer status could not be directly verified (bio subpages did not render additional detail either). Classified Freshman-friendly based on the visible pattern: every current MF's hometown is a direct-from-country listing (Ghana, Japan, Italy, Senegal, South Africa) consistent with Noonan's well-documented international club-academy recruiting network, with no transfer indicators anywhere on the roster. Lower confidence than other entries — flag for re-verification if a more detailed source is found.
minutesOutlook.trajectoryNote: Clemson is elite — 4 players went in round 1 of the 2026 draft. MF competition is among the toughest in college soccer. Olivier would likely be a development-year player in Year 1, building toward meaningful minutes by Year 2-3. The soccer development value is exceptional but immediate minutes are not guaranteed.

fin.internationalNote: Notre Dame meets 100% of demonstrated financial need for all admitted students. Athletic scholarship + need-based aid can substantially reduce cost. Requires financial disclosure.

culture.vibe: One of the most iconic university campuses in America — beautiful Gothic architecture, intense Catholic identity, football-dominant athletic culture. Soccer has real prestige as a national championship program. Small college town (South Bend) adjacent to campus.
culture.olivierMatch: ★★ Notre Dame is elite soccer and academics but cold Indiana winters are a significant negative for Olivier's warm-climate preference. Campus is not a city. However the quality of soccer development and the Notre Dame name carry enormous global prestige.
culture.socialScene: Strong campus social scene driven by football tailgates and athletic culture. International students in athletics. Beautiful campus for outdoor activities in fall. Cold winters limit outdoor lifestyle Oct-Mar.
culture.thingsToDo: South Bend is a modest mid-size city. Notre Dame campus is iconic and self-contained. Chicago (2hr) for big-city weekends. Lake Michigan beaches (1hr) in summer. Ann Arbor, MI (2hr).
culture.lifestyleTags: Cold winters, iconic campus, small city, elite academics

facilityDetails.academicLabs: Notre Dame pre-health program has excellent science facilities. University of Notre Dame health sciences labs. Clinical access through South Bend hospital network.
facilityDetails.sportsMed: Notre Dame Sports Medicine — full-time staff, team physicians. University of Notre Dame Health (Beacon Health System partnership) for clinical observation.
facilityDetails.extras: Notre Dame campus is one of the most beautiful in the US. Grotto of Our Lady iconic. Touchdown Jesus. Football gamedays are a unique experience. Chicago access for big-city weekends.

culture.olivierMatch: ★★★★ UNC is an excellent fit for Olivier — warm(ish) climate, college-town with city access (Raleigh 30min), elite Exercise Science degree explicitly designed for PT pathway, and top-25 soccer program. The main barrier is the selective admission and GPA 2.8.

facilityDetails.stadium: Dorrance Field — 4,200 chairback seats, dedicated soccer/lacrosse stadium opened 2019. Natural grass, LED video board, on-campus. Named after legendary coach Anson Dorrance.

facilityDetails.sportsScience: Full GPS tracking, video analysis, heart rate monitoring. UNC invests in sports science across all programs.

facilityDetails.sportsMed: UNC Sports Medicine — full-time staff. UNC Health (one of NC's top academic medical systems) and Duke Medical (Durham) proximity provide exceptional clinical observation access for pre-PT students.
facilityDetails.extras: Franklin Street, the Pit, Dean Dome — iconic UNC campus culture. ACC rivalry games (Duke, NC State). Research Triangle job market strongest in the Southeast. Blue Ridge Parkway hiking access.
